title: Samsung dates its foldable event, Gemini's big leap looms, and the AI you should actually use
intro: A week out from Galaxy Unpacked, Samsung's foldables get official; Gemini 3.5 Pro's rumored 2M-token jump is imminent; plus a genuinely useful Pixel trick and a plain-English guide to which AI fits which job.

posts.hook: Samsung's next foldable is officially dated
posts.body: Samsung has confirmed Galaxy Unpacked for July 22 in London, and the teaser — "A New Shape Unfolds" — all but confirms a shorter, wider Galaxy Z Fold 8 alongside the Z Flip 8, a new Galaxy Watch 9, One UI 9, next-gen Galaxy AI, and possibly Samsung's first smart glasses. This is the year foldables stop feeling like a flex and start feeling like a finished product. The interesting question isn't whether the hardware is thinner — it's whether the software finally makes the big inside screen worth the price.

Gemini 3.5 Pro is reportedly targeting a July 17 rollout with a rumored 2-million-token context window — double Gemini 3.5 Flash and the largest in any production frontier model. Worth flagging clearly: the date, the context number and the pricing are all unconfirmed by Google, and the model is still in limited enterprise preview. If it holds up, the headline isn't raw size — it's being able to drop an entire codebase or a stack of documents into one prompt without the model losing the thread.

posts.hook: Copy text your phone won't let you select
posts.body: On a Pixel (and most Android phones), you can grab text that an app blocks from selection. Open the Recent Apps switcher, then long-press the text directly inside the app's card preview — you can select and copy it from there. It's the fastest way to lift a tracking number, an OTP, or a quote out of an app that refuses to cooperate, no screenshot-and-retype required.

posts.hook: ChatGPT vs Gemini vs Claude — which for what?
posts.body: The honest answer is that no single model wins everything, so match the tool to the task. ChatGPT (GPT-5.6) is the fast, versatile daily driver for most people. Gemini 3.5 Pro shines when you're feeding it huge documents and need massive context. Claude Fable 5 leads on coding and careful, multi-step reasoning. Free tiers cover most everyday needs, so the smarter move is picking per-job rather than pledging loyalty to one app.

posts.hook: Foldable or slab in 2026 — which are you buying?
posts.body: With Unpacked a week away, the foldable-vs-slab question is live again. Foldables have never been thinner or more capable, but a great flat flagship still costs less and just works. If you were spending your own money this year, which way would you go — and what's the one thing stopping you from switching?
